spec.reasoning: This task involves transposing/reshaping data from a 'problem' sheet into a 'result' sheet. The risk flags indicate multi_sheet_references and data_values_match_headers, so we must be careful. The column headers 'A' and the multiline header in column B are actual data markers (letters A-H and numbers), not descriptive names — renaming them would change the data itself since 'A' appears both as a header and as data values throughout. Instead, I apply two safe perturbations: (1) Sheet renaming — 'problem' → 'source' and 'result' → 'output' — which tests whether the agent hardcodes sheet names. Both are single-word, similar descriptiveness level. (2) A distractor column 'C' inserted after the last column in the source sheet, which tests whether the agent correctly identifies the data boundary (columns A and B only) rather than assuming all columns contain data. The instruction references 'problem' and 'result' sheet names which will need updating to 'source' and 'output'. I avoid renaming column headers since 'A' appears as both a header and data values extensively, and the risk flag 'data_values_match_headers' warns about this. I also avoid blank_row_insert since the task depends on blank row positions (risk flag) and the data structure with multiline cells is sensitive to row changes.
